title: Review gate: re-derive machine findings, or read the digest file?
context: Issue #360 says the tightened ImplementationReviewGate fails when 'the digest contains' an unaddressed deterministic high/blocker finding. Reading .paqad/session/review-digest.md literally means the gate is inert whenever 'review digest' was not run, so skipping the verb silently disables the teeth. The alternative is one shared collector (rule report + duplication report + checks report + verification-evidence) used by BOTH the digest builder and the gate, so the gate reads the same cached JSON directly and cannot be bypassed. Same rows either way; the digest stays the model-facing input.

options.label: Shared collector: gate re-derives from the cached JSON (un-bypassable)

rationale: User chose the shared collector so the gate keeps its teeth when the digest verb is skipped; the digest stays the model-facing input, the collector is the single source of the rows.
